rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE EMILIANA IGNACIO RAMOS IRREVOCABLE SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/23/24. Prior to a review of the petition, the following must be addressed in a fully amended petition: 1) The accounting currently covers 10/18/23 to 10/24/23 (6 days). The accounting must be a one year accounting covering the period of 2/6/23 (date of order establishing the SNT) to 2/5/24. 2) The Inventory and Appraisal and the beginning of account assets must match. 3) Original bank statements for the end of account date (2/5/24) must be filed. 4) Notice of Hearing must be given to the beneficiary. 5) Proposed order must be e-filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL BARBARA HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required despite the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 12/19/2023), in light of the Court's prior order advancing the hearing date of this petition: 1) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver of notice signed by the decedent's brother. 2) Attachment 4c must be filed. 3) Attachment 7 must allege facts supporting the property characterization. If any asset is claimed to be community property, allegations required by LRSF 14.26(A) must also be set forth. 4) Attachment 7a must be filed to only identify the decedent's separate property and her one-half share of community property passing to the petitioner. 5) Attachment 7b must be filed to only identify one-half of community property belonging to the petitioner. 6) A copy of a statement of each financial account subject to this petition must be filed. The statement period must include the decedent's date of death. See LRSF 14.26(A). 7) The proposed order must include Attachment 7a to only identify one-half of community property belonging to the petitioner. Attachment 5a must be revised to only identify the decedent's separate property and her one-half share of community property passing to the petitioner. Note: To identify separate and community property interests in each financial account, either a specific dollar amount or a specific percentage of an account balance must be stated. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BYRON R. MEYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, Appointment Under IAEA
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The following issues remain unaddressed since the last hearing on 12/05/2023: 1) An amended DE-147 form must be filed to provide a missing telephone number and day of signing. 2) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 3) The petitioner's name stated at items 2 and 3 of the DE-121 form must exactly match the name under which this petition was filed (no middle initial). 4) The petition caption: The first box must be checked, as the petitioner is named as executor in the will. 5) Item 2b(1) must be checked. 6) The proof of service attached to the DE-121 form does not show direct notice given to Barbara Meyer. CRC 7.51(a)(1) & (2). No waiver of direct notice signed by her or her attorney is on file. Note: Depsite the parties' stipulation for appointment of Kevin Rodriguez as special administrator, no DE-147 and DE-147S forms have been filed by the proposed special administrator. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KENYATTA O. SCOTT
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/23/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/24/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 11/21/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-121 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 2) Re the proof of service attached to the DE-121 form: i) It must be dated and signed by a person other than the petitioner. See Probate Code section 1261 & CCP section 1013a. ii) Item 4a must specify the date of mailing. iii) Item 4b must specify the city and state from which required papers were mailed. 3) No proof of publication is on file. 4) Item 2a of the petition must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 5) Item 2b(3) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 6) Item 2d: At least one box must be checked. 7) Item 3d: i) Please provide a brief description of personal property. ii) If the estimated value of personal property does not exceed $184,500, please explain why the heirs are not proceeding with the small estate affidavit nonprobate procedure under Probate Code sections 13100 et seq. 8) Item 3e(4) must be unchecked, as it does not apply to the petitioner. If all other heirs waived the petitioner's bond, item 3e(3) must be checked, and DE-142 forms (bond waivers) signed by the heirs (other than the petitioner) must be filed. 9) Item 3g(2)(a) must be checked, if the decedent died intestate. 10) Item 3g(2)(c) must be checked, with the petitioner's relationship to the decedent specified. 11) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 13) Item 6b must be checked, if the decedent was survived by siblings. 14) Item 9: There were no attachments (4 pages) to the petition. 15) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. 16) A declaration required by LRSF 14.16(F) must be filed to state the total amount of anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate and describe such liabilities and claims. A minimum bond ($20,000) is required for a personal representative who does not reside in California, regardless of bond waivers and absence of any liabilities or claims.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SANDRA J. ZRNIC LIVING TRUST DATED JULY 27, 2022
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Confirm Assets Of A Trust
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/13/2024. 1) The proposed order must include the legal description of the property. 2) Copies of the DE-115 form and petition must be served on Michelle Rathbun at least 30 days before the hearing. See Probate Code sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(a)(2). The proof of service on file shows notice was given on 12/16/2023, less than 30 days before this hearing. New 30-day notice of the continued hearing must be given, absent a signed waiver. 3) Beverly Bratek must be served in the manner required by sections 851(b)(3) & 17203(b) and CCP sections 413.10 et seq. applicable to service of a civil summons. The mere mailing is not an authorized manner of service. See, e.g., CCP sections 415.30 and 415.40. 4) A copy of the supplement must be served. A proof of service must be filed. See CRC 7.53(b). 5) A declaration required by CRC 7.52(a) must be filed to describe efforts to identify and locate heirs of the predeceased spouse under section 6402.5(a)(1)-(3). 6) The legal description of the property set forth in the preliminary title report is different from the legal description contained in the petition. 7) The preliminary title report indicates that the petitioner as trustee already holds record title to one-half of the property. As such, the scopie of relief available under section 850(a)(3)(B) appears to be limited to the remaining one-half of the property titled in the predeceased spouse's name as his community property interest.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MILTON DEA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/21/2024, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 10/24/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 11/21/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-147 form is on file. 3) No DE-147S form is on file. This form must be filed confidentially and conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed or attached to any e-filed document). 4) No DE-121 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 5) No proof of publication is on file. 6) The petition caption: The IAEA box must be checked. 7) Item 2a must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 8) Item 3a: The decedent died more than 4 years ago. Please briefly explain why the petitioner delayed opening this probate. 9) Item 3e(3): No bond waiver (DE-142 form) signed by the decedent's sister is on file. Item 4 of the DE-142 form must specify the petitioner's name. 10) Item 3g(1)(c) must be unchecked, if the decedent died intestate. 11) Item 3h(3) must be checked. 12) Item 5a(3) or (4) must be checked. 13) Item 5a(7) or (8) must be checked. 14) Item 8: Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. Note: An appearance may be required on 02/21/2024 for the Court to consider whether to take this petition off calendar if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.
